qa_pairs.question: What are some important command-line options for `cfx5solve` in Ansys CFX simulations, and how do they affect performance or resource usage?
qa_pairs.answer: A summary of command-line options can be printed by running `cfx5solve -help`. By default, `cfx5solve` runs in single precision; to run in double precision, add the `-double` option, which will also double memory requirements. For larger meshes, supporting up to 2 billion elements (compared to the default 80 million structured or 200 million unstructured), the `-large` option can be used. These options can be specified for the Partitioner, Interpolator, or Solver.

qa_pairs.question: How can Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections be automated in Ansys Fluent using journal file commands, and what does a basic steady injection file look like?
qa_pairs.answer: DPM injections can be automated by adding commands to the journal file after solution initialization, such as `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1`, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no`. A basic manually created steady injection file, like `zinjection01.inj`, would include parameters such as `x`, `y`, `z` coordinates, `u`, `v`, `w` velocities, `diameter`, `t` (temperature), `mass-flow`, `mass`, `frequency`, `time`, and a `name` for each injection point.

qa_pairs.question: How can one submit a multinode Ansys CFX job using a Slurm script, and what are the key configurations for different Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit a multinode Ansys CFX job using a Slurm script, you must specify the account, time limit, `--nodes` (two or more), and `--ntasks-per-node` (e.g., `192` to use all cores). After loading `StdEnv/2023` and an `ansys` module (e.g., `ansys/2023R2`), the `NNODES` variable is determined using `slurm_hl2hl.py --format ANSYS-CFX`. For clusters like `narval` or `fir`, the `cfx5solve` command uses `-start-method "Open MPI Distributed Parallel" -par-dist $NNODES`. For other clusters, it uses `Intel MPI Distributed Parallel`, requiring `export I_MPI_HYDRA_BOOTSTRAP=ssh` and `unset I_MPI_HYDRA_BOOTSTRAP_EXEC_EXTRA_ARGS`.
